title: Neottia nanchuanica
intro: Neottia nanchuanica, also known as the Nanchuan Toad Lily, is a species of orchid native to the mountains of southwestern China. It has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders. Bioactive constituents of Neottia nanchuanica include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Neottia nanchuanica is often prepared as a decoction or an extract, or added to other herbal remedies, such as the 'Jade Screen Powder' and 'Huang Qi Tang', to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Neottia nanchuanica is classified as a species of orchid. It belongs to the genus Neottia, which is a group of terrestrial, leafless orchids commonly known as the 'ghost orchids'. Neottia nanchuanica is further classified within the family Orchidaceae, which comprises one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants. The species is also classified as being endemic to China, indicating that it is found only in this country. This classification helps to understand the evolutionary relationships and conservation status of Neottia nanchuanica.
